name: H.E. Leandro Rizzuto
title: Ambassador
organization: Permanent Mission of the United States to the OAS

name: H.E. Raul Florentin Antola
title: Ambassador
organization: Permanent Mission of Paraguay to the OAS
bio: H.E. Raul Florentin Antola is Paraguay’s Permanent Representative to the Organization of American States (OAS) and concurrent Ambassador to The Bahamas and Saint Kitts and Nevis. In his current role, he represents Paraguay in the principal organs of the OAS, participating in multilateral negotiations, promoting regional cooperation, and advancing initiatives related to economic development, innovation, and entrepreneurship across the Americas. His work includes engaging in policy dialogue, fostering partnerships, and supporting programs that enhance trade, investment, and institutional capacity. He previously served as Ambassador of Paraguay to Japan and Germany, where he strengthened bilateral economic relations and cooperation frameworks. He also held senior national positions, including Director of the Investment and Export Network (REDIEX) at the Ministry of Industry and Commerce, and Director General of Protocol at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

name: Alberto Sborovsky
title: Vice Minister of Trade and Services
organization: Ministry of Industry and Commerce of Paraguay
bio: In 2026, I assumed the position of Vice Minister of trade and services. I come from the private sector, specifically from the food industry and supermarkets. I have held representative positions in key industry associations, such as President of the Paraguayan Chamber of Supermarkets (CAPASU) and Vice President for International Affairs of the Paraguayan Industrial Union (UIP). My expertise lies in commercial strategy and in-depth knowledge of the consumer market.

name: H.E. Edison Lanza
title: Ambassador
organization: Permanent Mission of Uruguay to OAS
bio: Edison Lanza is a graduate from the Law School of the University of the Republic (Uruguay). Expertise in public international law, human rights and freedom of expression, government affairs (1988). President of Inter-American Council for Integral Development (CIDI)-OAS. Director of International Relations and Open Government of the Departmental Government of Canelones, Uruguay (2021-2025). Special Rapporteur for Freedom of Expression of the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, leading the office principal monitor for freedom of expression and right to information in the Americas (10/2014 – 10/2020). Appointed as a senior fellow of the Peter D. Bell Rule of Law Program at Inter American Dialogue (2020 to date). Appointed by American University Washington College of Law, Center for Justice and International Law (CEJIL) and Due Process of Law Foundation (DPLF) as a member of an Independent Panel to Observe and Report on the Selection Processes of the candidates for Commissioners of the Interamerican Commission on Human Rights of the Organization of American States (OAS, IACHR) (Washington, May 2023 to present).
